skos:Concept.skos:scopeNote: View across roofs, generally in an urban context, showing details of roof and chimney construction.
Heart failure and various heart conditions.
Ramayana Week.
Complete and parial hearing loss.
Glaucoma, diabetes, cataracts, retinal detachment and other related conditions.
Sporting venues and competitions in progress.
For example Binge eating, Anorexia Nervosa and Bullemia.
The Festival of Sacrifice.
For example chronic heart failure, high blood pressure, congenital heart disease, heart transplants, rheumatic fever and other related illnesses.
Jewish New Year.
For example asthma, Cystic Fibrosis, Pneumoconiosis, Bronchieactasis, mycotic and other chronic infections, sleep related breathing disorders and lung transplant.
For example chronic liver disease, inflammatory bowel disease, weight loss, liver transplant and other related illnesses.
The festival for the first day after Ramadan.
Seaborne assault landing troops ashore with resultant fighting on land, examples being the Battle of Maldon, 991, the Normandy landings of WWII and so on.
For example Asbestosis, ME/Chronic Fatigue Syndrome, HIV, Lupus, Lyme Disease, MS, Overactive Thyroid Disease, Pancreatitis and other related illnesses.
For example allergic contact dermatitis, Barlows Syndrome, cutaneous melanoma, irritant contact dermatitis, MRSA infection, mummification and other related illnesses.

Raid or assault by ships upon a terrestrial location, typically a settlement or town, which may involve a terrestrial incursion and damage to buildings and/or capture of people. It is distinct from a battle or naval skirmish.
For example tumors, leukemia, bone/ breast/ colon/ prostate cancer, Hodgings Lymphoma/ Disease, oral/ throat cancer, melanoma, ovarian and pancreatic cancer and other related illnesses.
Urban road or open space with typical activity, traffic and pedestrians. Double indexing may be appropriate with e.g. TRADE AND COMMERCE or CONSTRUCTION.
Engagement between any combination of warship, privateer, or commercial vessel in a time of war but not itself a battle. Such skirmishes may precede or follow naval battles.
Depicting the nature and arrangement of strets and buildings within a built-up or urban area. For views within a singel street use STREET SCENE.
Chronic kidney disease, diabetes, thyroid illnesses.
Festival of Lots
Views across or along the sea and seafront, lakes and rivers.
Festival of Lights
Scenes of snowfall or lying snow.

For example, spinal tumor, stroke, Alzheimers, Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD), Cerebral Palsy, Chronic Fatigue, Dementia, Distal Muscular Dystrophy, Huntingtons Disease, Multiple Sclerosis, Narcolepsy and Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder.
Pentecost.
The Festival of Lights.
Views achieved from an aircraft or other high location.
Movements of men, munitions, and supplies in support either of naval manoeuvres and engagements or of terrestrial troop movements at home and abroad.
The month of pilgrimage to Mecca.

For example arthritis, club foot, back pain, amputations and loss of certain functions. Major dysfunction of a joint, reconstructive surgery, disorder of the spine, amputations, fractures of extremities and growth impairments.

For example mood disorders, agoraphobia, anxiety, bipolar disorder, chronic depression, eating disorders, dissociative identity disorder, multiple personality disorder, obsessive-compulsive or paranoid personality disorder and schizophrenia.
Combined physical and mental health difficulties or conditions.
Day of Atonement.
Hindu festival dedicated to Shiva.
Passover.
Loss of speech and other forms of speech disorder.
Hematological disorders such as chronic anemia, hemophilia and acute leukemia.
The Islamic New Year.
Scenes viewed at night.
Celebrates worship of the Hindu goddess Durga.
